alignments.preprint_claim_text: The repeated resetting of weights in the last layer, termed 'zapping', leads to representations that exhibit better continual and transfer learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The repeated resetting of weights in the last layer, termed 'zapping', leads to representations that exhibit better continual and transfer learning.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim verbatim about zapping improving continual and transfer learning, so content, scope, and confidence are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The zapping procedure results in improved transfer accuracy and/or more rapid adaptation in both standard fine-tuning and continual learning settings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The zapping procedure results in improved transfer accuracy and/or more rapid adaptation in both standard fine-tuning and continual learning settings.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this result with equivalent wording and emphasis, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Zapping achieves performance on par with state of the art meta-learning without needing expensive higher-order gradients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The zapping approach may be considered a computationally cheaper alternative to meta-learning that enables rapidly adaptable features without needing expensive higher-order gradients.
alignments.rationale: The publication softens the original assertion (from achieving parity to 'may be considered' a cheaper alternative), reducing the strength of the equivalence claim while keeping the same comparison to meta-learning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Representations trained with repeated zapping learn features that are capable of rapidly adapting to newly initialized classifiers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Representations trained with repeated zapping learn features that are capable of rapidly adapting to newly initialized classifiers.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this conclusion with equivalent content and confidence, so no substantive change is observed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The zapping mechanism invites further investigation into the usefulness of resetting neural network parameters during training.
alignments.rationale: This forward-looking, low-certainty suggestion from the preprint does not appear in the publication's listed claims and thus is treated as removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed semantic-forward (SF) relaying framework reduces forwarding payload and improves network robustness against intra-link errors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed semantic-forward (SF) relaying framework reduces forwarding payload and improves network robustness against intra-link errors.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this contribution with effectively identical wording and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A joint source-channel coding algorithm is designed to enhance decoding gains at the destination by iteratively exchanging extrinsic information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A joint source-channel coding algorithm is designed to iteratively exchange extrinsic information to enhance decoding gains at the destination.
alignments.rationale: The publication's phrasing conveys the same algorithmic contribution and mechanism; differences are minor wording only.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Simulation results indicate that SF relaying can effectively improve the recovered information quality even in bad channel conditions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Simulation results indicate that SF relaying can effectively improve the recovered information quality even in bad channel conditions.
alignments.rationale: The simulation result is carried over unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Automated visual firearms classification from RGB images can significantly aid in identifying criminal firearms trafficking networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Automated visual firearms classification from RGB images can significantly aid in identifying criminal firearms trafficking networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this high-level motivation/contribution verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep Neural Networks (DNN) are the state-of-the-art methodology for visual firearms classification.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate a general claim that DNNs are the SOTA; instead it focuses on experiments with SSL and ViT, so the preprint's broad SOTA claim is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The common transfer learning approach involves pretraining on a large-scale dataset like ImageNet-1k before finetuning on a task-specific dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes a new mixed pretraining objective rather than reiterating the generic ImageNet-pretrain-then-finetune description, so the preprint statement is not present as a standalone claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Visual Transformer (ViT) neural architectures and Self-Supervised Learning (SSL) approaches have not been evaluated for visual firearms classification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental results indicate the superiority of certain Self-Supervised Learning pretraining methods that cooperate well with the Visual Transformer architecture.
alignments.rationale: The preprint claimed a lack of evaluation, but the publication includes experiments evaluating ViT+SSL and reports their superiority, thus reversing and strengthening the original limitation claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CausalVul consistently improved the model accuracy, robustness, and OOD performance for all the state-of-the-art models and datasets we experimented.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CausalVul consistently improved the model accuracy, robustness and OOD performance for all the state-of-the-art models and datasets we experimented.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this empirical result with equivalent wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model is not robust under perturbation and cannot generalize well over out-of-distribution data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model is not robust under perturbation and it cannot generalize well over the out-of-distribution (OOD) data.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this conclusion verbatim, so no change is detected.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This is the first work that introduces do calculus based causal learning to software engineering models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This is the first work that introduces do calculus based causal learning to software engineering models.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The trained Bayesian neural networks can estimate parameters of the gamma process and provide uncertainty through the estimation of parameters of a probability distribution.

alignments.pub_claim_text: NoxTrader's prediction data has an impressive correlation range between 0.65 and 0.75.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of filtering techniques improved the initial -60% investment return to 325%.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) and Large Language Models (LLMs) have the potential to create deepfakes that are indistinguishable from reality.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The misuse of GenAI and LLMs can lead to the orchestration of malicious campaigns and targeted misinformation.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The societal implications of GenAI applications include the blurring of lines between virtual and real worlds.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is an urgent need to recognize and address the challenges posed by the potential nefarious applications of GenAI.

alignments.rationale: The preprint's call to urgent action is represented in the publication as a research synthesis proposing preparation strategies; this reframes the urgency into a constructive contribution and reports slightly lower certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: AI-powered botnets on social media platforms represent a significant risk associated with GenAI.
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces an explicit claim about AI-powered botnets that does not appear in the preprint, so this is an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed brain-inspired platform enables real-time event-based Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs) for processing sensory signals.

alignments.rationale: The publication reframes sensory signal processing as continuous data processing at the edge, broadening the application context while preserving the real-time SNN capability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The system supports the emulation of various neural processing phenomena, including short-term plasticity and spike frequency adaptation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system supports the emulation of various neural processing phenomena, including short-term plasticity and spike frequency adaptation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The asynchronous infrastructure of the system allows for the definition of different network architectures and provides event-based interfaces for data conversion.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The asynchronous infrastructure of the system allows for the definition of different network architectures and provides event-based interfaces for data conversion.
alignments.rationale: The infrastructure and interface claims are restated identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The flexibility of the chip to emulate biologically plausible neural networks facilitates the development and validation of complex models of neural processing.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The flexibility of the chip to emulate biologically plausible neural networks facilitates the development and validation of complex models for neural processing.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same conclusion about the chip's flexibility and its role in model development, with only minor wording changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: QGANs can generate high-quality quantum data with a fidelity higher than 90%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: QGANs can generate high-quality quantum data with a fidelity higher than 90% even under conditions where up to half of the generator's phase shifters are damaged. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the >90% fidelity result but explicitly situates it under damaged-phase-shifter conditions (merging with the damage claim), thus clarifying and broadening the original claim's applicability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: QGANs can perform learning tasks on near-term quantum devices affected by noise and defects. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work demonstrates the feasibility of implementing QGANs on NISQ-era quantum hardware. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's conclusion as a stronger demonstration of feasibility on NISQ hardware, increasing certainty while preserving the original conclusion about near-term device applicability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of QGANs remains effective even when up to half of the generator's phase shifters are damaged. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: QGANs can generate high-quality quantum data with a fidelity higher than 90% even under conditions where up to half of the generator's phase shifters are damaged. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly includes this damaged-phase-shifter robustness in Q0 with the same content and certainty, so the claim is effectively unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: QGANs can operate under conditions where all of the generator and discriminator's phase shifters are subjected to phase noise up to 0.04π. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: QGANs can still perform effectively when all of the generator and discriminator's phase shifters are subjected to phase noise up to 0.04π. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this result with equivalent wording and certainty, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work sheds light on the feasibility of implementing QGANs on NISQ-era quantum hardware. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work demonstrates the feasibility of implementing QGANs on NISQ-era quantum hardware. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a stronger, more definitive statement ('demonstrates' and higher certainty) about feasibility compared to the preprint's more cautious framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PACIA obtains the state-of-the-art performance in few-shot molecular property prediction problems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim corresponds to the PACIA few-shot molecular property SOTA result; the publication addresses different methods and problems, so this claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed hierarchical adaptation mechanism is rational and effective. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication's claims focus on PML feature selection and related issues rather than the hierarchical adaptation mechanism, so this preprint conclusion is not represented.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The increase of adaptive parameters can lead to overfitting and poor performance. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The 'Curse of dimensionality' increases the risk of model overfitting and leads to a decline in model classification performance. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's parameter-specific overfitting limitation in broader terms as the curse of dimensionality, generalizing the concern and stating it with higher certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The automatic sleep analysis achieved a comparable level of agreement with manual analysis across different sleep recording types. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The channel-wise ensemble model demonstrated robustness and the ability to generalise across various types of sleep recordings, including novel self-applied home polysomnography. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the model architecture (channel-wise ensemble) and explicitly highlights generalisation to novel recording types, expanding and clarifying the preprint's result about comparable agreement across recording types.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Validation of the gray area concept revealed its potential to enhance sleep staging accuracy and identify areas in the recordings where sleep technologists struggle to reach a consensus. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The validation of the grey area concept revealed its potential to enhance sleep staging accuracy and to highlight regions in the recordings where sleep experts may struggle to reach a consensus. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study introduces and validates a concept from explainable artificial intelligence into sleep medicine. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed automatic sleep staging model (aSAGA) effectively utilises both electroencephalography and electro-oculography channels while incorporating transparency of uncertainty in the decision-making process. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed human-in-the-loop concept for sleep analysis aims to reduce black-box criticism and the burden associated with manual sleep staging. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study provides a technical basis and understanding of automatic sleep staging uncertainty, which has the potential to improve the integration of automatic sleep staging into clinical practice. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis conducted in this study aims to help future researchers increase the use of machine learning in assessing Wikipedia article quality.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis conducted in this study aims to help future researchers increase the use of machine learning in assessing Wikipedia article quality.
alignments.rationale: The stated aim to assist future researchers is preserved verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Anomalous diffusion is present at all scales, from atomic to large scales.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Anomalous diffusion is present at all scales, from atomic to large ones.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same general conclusion with equivalent scope and confidence; wording differs only slightly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new data-driven method utilizing Gramian Angular Fields (GAF) allows for the encoding of one-dimensional trajectories as images while preserving their spatiotemporal structure.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new data-driven method utilizing Gramian angular fields (GAF) can effectively encode one-dimensional trajectories as images while preserving their spatiotemporal structure.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is described equivalently in both versions; minor wording differences do not change meaning or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using GAF images enables us to outperform the current state-of-the-art in characterizing underlying diffusive regimes and inferring the anomalous diffusion exponent.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The GAF images can outperform the current state-of-the-art methods for characterizing underlying diffusive regimes.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the outperforming claim for characterizing regimes but drops the explicit claim about inferring the anomalous diffusion exponent, narrowing the scope and tempering the overall claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Short raw trajectories, of lengths between 10 and 50, are the most difficult to characterize in single-particle tracking experiments.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not reproduce this limitation statement; instead it introduces a different claim about accessibility for short trajectories, so the original difficulty-focused claim is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Self-supervised methods for depth prediction struggle with edges, self-occlusion, and lighting inconsistency due to unreliable loss functions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Self-supervised methods for depth prediction struggle with edges, self-occlusion, and lighting inconsistency.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same limitation; minor wording difference does not alter the substantive claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Methods trained on synthetic data can accurately predict depth for synthetic geometries but tend to overfit to synthetic anatomies and properties.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Methods trained on synthetic data can overfit to synthetic anatomies and properties, providing inaccurate depth for real data.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the practical consequence (inaccurate real-data depth) making the limitation clearer while preserving the original point about overfitting.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach leverages both labeled synthetic and unlabeled real data to improve depth prediction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach leverages both labeled synthetic and unlabeled real data to improve depth prediction.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel approach results in more resilient and accurate depth maps of real colonoscopy sequences.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel approach results in more resilient and accurate depth maps of real colonoscopy sequences.
alignments.rationale: The reported result is preserved verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our model-adaptation approach improves recognition accuracy while reducing data requirements.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our model-adaptation approach improves recognition accuracy while reducing data requirements.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the contribution without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method enhances the safety and convenience of in-vehicle interactions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method enhances the safety and convenience of in-vehicle interactions.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about safety and convenience is preserved with the same emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We achieve recognition accuracy of up to 90% with our approach.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We achieve recognition accuracy of up to 90% with our approach.
alignments.rationale: The quantitative result is identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Personalized adaptation and incremental learning are effective for a user-centered design.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Personalized adaptation and incremental learning are effective for a user-centered design.
alignments.rationale: The claim content is the same, but the publication reports this result with lower certainty (changed from high to medium), so confidence is marked as tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: VAL is an interactive task learning system that integrates large language models with symbolic reasoning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: VAL is an interactive task learning system that integrates large language models for specific tasks within an algorithmic framework.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the integration emphasis (algorithmic framework / specific tasks) versus the preprint's mention of symbolic reasoning; this is a clarification of the system description rather than a substantive reversal.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: VAL allows for incremental knowledge acquisition from limited human instruction in natural language.
alignments.pub_claim_text: VAL allows for the interactive learning of hierarchical task knowledge from natural language, resulting in human interpretable knowledge.
alignments.rationale: The publication elaborates that the learning is interactive and hierarchical and emphasizes the resulting interpretability, refining the original incremental acquisition claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The knowledge acquired by VAL is human interpretable and generalizes to support execution of novel tasks without additional training.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The knowledge acquired by VAL generalizes to support the execution of novel tasks without additional training.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the generalization claim but down-weights the interpretability emphasis and lowers certainty (high->medium), so the claim is clarified and confidence tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Most users could successfully teach VAL using language they felt was natural in a video game setting.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Most users could successfully teach VAL using language they felt was natural in a video game setting.
alignments.rationale: The user-study result is preserved but reported with higher certainty in the publication (medium->high), representing an amplification of confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Differentiable Programming (DP) produces the most accurate gradients under Laplace and Navier-Stokes equations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Differentiable Programming (DP) produces the most accurate gradients under Laplace and Navier-Stokes equations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is effectively identical in wording and meaning to the preprint claim, with no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DP thrives even when Direct-Adjoint Looping (DAL) fails and Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) struggle. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DP thrives even when Direct-Adjoint Looping (DAL) fails and Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) struggle. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim language and implications are the same in preprint and publication, with no detectable change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides a detailed benchmark highlighting the limited conditions under which DAL, PINNs, and DP can be efficiently used. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study provides a detailed benchmark highlighting the limited conditions under which DAL, PINNs, and DP can be efficiently used. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is presented the same way in both versions; wording and scope are consistent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The work serves as a guide to Optimal Control practitioners and connects them further to the Deep Learning community. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The work serves as a guide to Optimal Control practitioners and connects them further to the Deep Learning community. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion and intended audience linkage remain the same between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed speed prediction method is independent of large historical speed data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed speed prediction method is independent of large historical speed data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this methodological contribution verbatim, with no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The trajectory road topographical features are used to fit a Shared Weight Multilayer Perceptron learning model for predicting vehicle speed. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of trajectory road topographical features to fit a Shared Weight Multilayer Perceptron learning model leads to significant improvement over standard regression analysis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication combines the model description with the performance outcome, making the link between feature/model choice and improved performance more explicit (clarification rather than scope change).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results demonstrate significant improvement, both qualitative and quantitative, over standard regression analysis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of trajectory road topographical features to fit a Shared Weight Multilayer Perceptron learning model leads to significant improvement over standard regression analysis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same performance improvement, merging it with the model description but keeping the substantive result intact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework provides new insights for designing approaches for traffic analysis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework provides new insights for designing approaches for traffic analysis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The high-level conclusion remains the same in the published version with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method, Expert enhanced dynamic time warping anomaly detection (E-DTWA), improves anomaly detection by incorporating a human-in-the-loop concept. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method, Expert enhanced dynamic time warping anomaly detection (E-DTWA), improves anomaly detection in time series data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Both claims assert that E-DTWA improves anomaly detection; the publication emphasizes time-series context but preserves the improvement claim from the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: E-DTWA offers efficient detection of anomalies while maintaining low computational and space complexity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: E-DTWA maintains low computational and space complexity while improving explainability in anomaly detection. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the low complexity claim but adds explainability and lowers stated certainty, so the claim is clarified and confidence is tempered relative to the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method allows for flexible retraining based on expert feedback, enhancing the detection process. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach allows for flexible retraining based on expert feedback, which contributes to its efficiency. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the flexible retraining property but expresses it with higher certainty and links it explicitly to efficiency, increasing confidence in the claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: E-DTWA incorporates a human-in-the-loop concept to enhance the anomaly detection process. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication separates and highlights the human-in-the-loop aspect as a standalone claim; while implied in the preprint, it appears as an explicit additional publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study establishes the feasibility of a robust experimental model and deep learning solution for UWB microwave stroke detection. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study establishes the feasibility of a robust experimental model and deep learning solution for ultra-wideband microwave stroke detection. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the feasibility conclusion with only a minor wording change (ultra-wideband spelled out), preserving meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An overall sensitivity and specificity for detection greater than 0.99 was observed. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An overall sensitivity and specificity for detection greater than 0.99 was observed. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric performance result is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A Rayleigh mean localization error of 1.65 mm was achieved in the detection of hemorrhage. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A Rayleigh mean localization error of 1.65 mm was achieved in the detection of hemorrhage. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The localization error result is unchanged in wording and magnitude.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Low-energy microwaves have potential as low-cost, small form factor, fast, and safe probes of tissue dielectric properties. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Low-energy microwaves have potential for low-cost and fast tissue dielectric properties measurements. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The published claim omits ‘small form factor’ and ‘safe’ compared with the preprint, reducing the scope of the asserted potential while keeping overall confidence similar.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Selenite produced accurate and high-quality overviews reliably. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Selenite produced accurate and high-quality overviews reliably. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is reproduced verbatim in the publication with no substantive changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Selenite significantly accelerated users' information processing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Selenite significantly accelerated users’ information processing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Selenite effectively improved users' overall comprehension and sensemaking experience. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Selenite effectively improved users' overall comprehension and sensemaking experience. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this user-experience improvement claim without modification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method DANI infers the underlying network while preserving its structural properties.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DANI preserves essential structural properties such as modular structure, degree distribution, connected components, density, and clustering coefficients.
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert that DANI preserves the same set of structural/topological properties; the publication restates the preservation claim without changing meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DANI demonstrates higher accuracy and lower run time compared to well-known network inference methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DANI exhibits higher accuracy and lower run time compared to well-known network inference methods.
alignments.rationale: The result claim is the same in content, but the publication downgrades certainty (high->medium), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DANI maintains critical topological characteristics such as modular structure, degree distribution, connected components, density, and clustering coefficients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DANI preserves essential structural properties such as modular structure, degree distribution, connected components, density, and clustering coefficients.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same list of preserved topological characteristics, matching the preprint claim closely.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method has linear time complexity and is scalable in a distributed MapReduce framework.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The presented method has linear time complexity, increasing with the number of nodes, cascades, and the square of the average length of cascades.
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the complexity statement with specific dependencies and omits the explicit MapReduce scalability claim, narrowing the original scalability assertion.

alignments.pub_claim_text: DANI is constructed using the Markov transition matrix derived from the analysis of time series cascades and node-node similarity in cascade behavior.
alignments.rationale: This contribution-level detail about construction (Markov transition matrix and cascade analysis) does not appear in the preprint and is newly presented in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: All models achieved high accuracy, indicating their ability to accurately learn and classify chicken eggs' fertility state.
alignments.pub_claim_text: All models achieved high accuracy during training, indicating their ability to accurately learn and classify chicken eggs’ fertility state.
alignments.rationale: Both claims state that all models achieved high accuracy for the fertility classification task with consistent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: InceptionNet exhibited the best overall performance, accurately classifying fertile and non-fertile eggs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: InceptionNet demonstrated excellent performance, with an accuracy of 0.9804, a sensitivity of 1 for detecting fertile eggs, and a specificity of 0.9615 for identifying non-fertile eggs.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides concrete performance numbers that clarify and quantify the preprint's assertion that InceptionNet was best-performing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: InceptionNet achieved an accuracy of 0.98, a sensitivity of 1 for detecting fertile eggs, and a specificity of 0.96 for identifying non-fertile eggs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: InceptionNet demonstrated excellent performance, with an accuracy of 0.9804, a sensitivity of 1 for detecting fertile eggs, and a specificity of 0.9615 for identifying non-fertile eggs.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents effectively the same performance metrics (more precise numeric values), so the claim is unchanged in substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The higher performance of InceptionNet is attributed to its unique architecture efficiently capturing features at different scales.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate the attribution of InceptionNet's performance to its architecture; this explanatory claim is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Further optimization and fine-tuning of the models might be necessary to address the limitations in accurately detecting fertile and non-fertile eggs in case of other models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Further optimization and fine-tuning of the models are necessary to address the limitations in accurately detecting fertile and non-fertile eggs.
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the recommendation (from 'might be necessary' to 'are necessary'), increasing confidence in the need for further optimization.

alignments.pub_claim_text: VGG16 and ResNet50 struggled to identify fertile and non-fertile eggs, as suggested by NaN values for sensitivity and specificity.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds specific negative results about VGG16 and ResNet50 that were not present in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: This study highlights the potential of CNN-Transfer Learning for nondestructive fertility detection.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames a broader contribution (potential of CNN-transfer learning) that was not explicitly claimed in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Spider4SPARQL is a new SPARQL benchmark dataset featuring 9,693 manually generated NL questions and 4,721 unique, novel, and complex SPARQL queries.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Spider4SPARQL is a new SPARQL benchmark dataset featuring 9,693 manually generated NL questions and 4,721 unique, novel, and complex SPARQL queries.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the exact contribution claim about dataset size and characteristics.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The benchmark includes 166 knowledge graphs and ontologies covering 138 different domains.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The benchmark includes 166 knowledge graphs and ontologies covering 138 different domains.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same dataset coverage claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: State-of-the-art KGQA systems and LLMs achieve only up to 45% execution accuracy when evaluated with Spider4SPARQL.
alignments.pub_claim_text: State-of-the-art KGQA systems and LLMs achieve only up to 45% execution accuracy when evaluated with Spider4SPARQL.
alignments.rationale: The reported evaluation result is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The pattern-based generation approaches of existing benchmarks do not generalize well to the vague and linguistically diverse questions asked by humans.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The pattern-based generation approaches of existing benchmarks do not generalize well to the vague and linguistically diverse questions asked by humans.
alignments.rationale: The limitation claim is repeated verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Language models contribute to accelerating the molecule discovery cycle in early-stage drug discovery.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Language models contribute to accelerating the molecule discovery cycle in early-stage drug discovery.
alignments.rationale: Both versions assert the same high-level result about language models accelerating early-stage molecule discovery.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The strength of language models in molecular discovery includes de novo drug design, property prediction, and reaction chemistry.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The chapter reviews the strengths and weaknesses of language models in de novo drug design, property prediction, and reaction chemistry.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the claim as a balanced review (strengths and weaknesses) rather than only stating strengths, clarifying the scope of discussion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Open-source software assets lower the entry barrier to the field of scientific language modeling.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Open-source software assets are highlighted to lower the entry barrier to the field of scientific language modeling.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the same contribution about open-source assets lowering entry barriers.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The authors propose a vision for future molecular design that combines a chatbot interface with access to computational chemistry tools.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A vision for future molecular design is outlined, integrating a chatbot interface with computational chemistry tools through retrieval-augmented generation techniques.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides additional technical detail (retrieval-augmented generation) clarifying how the proposed chatbot-integration vision could be realized.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new decision support system provides insights for better exploration of a brand's image and improvement of its connectivity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new decision support system provides insights for better exploration of a brand’s image and improvement of its connectivity.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim about providing insights for brand image and connectivity improvement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The goal of improving connectivity is achieved by solving an extended version of the Maximum Betweenness Improvement problem.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed algorithmic framework solves an extended version of the Maximum Betweenness Improvement problem.
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert that the algorithmic framework addresses an extended Maximum Betweenness Improvement problem; meaning is consistent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new algorithmic framework is integrated into a software system called Brand Network Booster (BNB).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Brand Network Booster (BNB) software system supports brand connectivity evaluation and improvement.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the software's functionality (evaluation and improvement) whereas the preprint emphasized integration; this clarifies the tool's role.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Brand Network Booster (BNB) supports brand connectivity evaluation and improvement.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Brand Network Booster (BNB) software system supports brand connectivity evaluation and improvement.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same functional claim about BNB supporting evaluation and improvement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The tool and approach are valuable for network scholars and facilitate strategic decision-making for marketing and communication managers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The tool and approach are valuable for facilitating strategic decision-making processes for marketing and communication managers.
alignments.rationale: The publication omits the explicit mention of "network scholars," narrowing the target audience while keeping the managerial decision-making claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed GAN-based synthetic-image generator improves retrieval performance in night-time image domains by augmenting training data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed GAN-based synthetic-image generator translates available day-time image examples into night images to augment training data in domains with limited availability. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the augmentation benefit but is more specific (translating day images and targeting domains with limited availability), narrowing the original general phrasing while preserving the claim of improved retrieval.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel light-weight GAN architecture is introduced that enforces consistency between original and translated images through edge consistency. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel light-weight GAN architecture is introduced that enforces consistency between the original and translated images through edge consistency. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this architectural contribution verbatim, preserving meaning and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method achieves state-of-the-art results on the Tokyo 24/7 day-night retrieval benchmark while maintaining performance on Oxford and Paris datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method of diverse anchor mining improves over the state-of-the-art results on a standard Tokyo 24/7 day-night retrieval benchmark. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the Tokyo 24/7 SOTA claim but frames it specifically as due to diverse anchor mining; the separate publication claim about Oxford/Paris is presented elsewhere (see added claim).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method allows for simultaneous training of an edge detector that operates on both night and day images. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel light-weight GAN architecture is introduced that enforces consistency between the original and translated images through edge consistency. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes edge consistency in the GAN architecture but does not state simultaneous training of an edge detector; the original operational claim is weakened/omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed diverse anchor mining method increases variability in training examples and maximizes the generalization of the trained model. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method of diverse anchor mining improves over the state-of-the-art results on a standard Tokyo 24/7 day-night retrieval benchmark. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's generalization/variability claim into a concrete performance improvement on the Tokyo benchmark (narrower but reported with higher certainty).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed basic top-down approach to splitting is proven to be equivalent to the bottom-up approach. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed basic top-down approach to splitting is proven to be equivalent to the bottom-up approach. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this theoretical equivalence unchanged in phrasing and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new definition of the extended approach is provably applicable to many of the existing semantics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new extended approach is provably applicable to many of the existing semantics for epistemic logic programs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the provable applicability claim with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The extended approach operates similarly to traditional Answer Set Programming (ASP). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The extended approach operates similarly to traditional answer set programming. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this comparison to traditional ASP unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The extended approach provably coincides with the bottom-up notion of splitting at least on the class of Epistemically Stratified Programs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The extended approach coincides with the bottom-up notion of splitting for Epistemically Stratified Programs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication states the same coincidence result for the stated program class without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The extended approach better adheres to common ASP programming methodology. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The extended approach better adheres to common answer set programming methodology. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this methodological-conclusion claim with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep neural network model identified patients without indication of AF in the presented ECG but who will develop AF in the future with an AUC score of 0.845. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep neural network model identified patients without indication of AF in the presented ECG but who will develop AF in the future with an AUC score of 0.845. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the reported AUC result and claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Patients in the high-risk group are 50% more likely to develop AF within 40 weeks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Patients in the high-risk group are 50% more likely to develop AF within 40 weeks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this relative-risk finding unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Patients belonging to the minimal-risk group have more than 85% chance of remaining AF free up until after seven years. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Patients belonging to the minimal-risk group have over 85% chance of remaining AF free up until after seven years. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this long-term minimal-risk probability claim with equivalent wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed model for AF risk prediction possesses the potential of providing valuable and useful information in decision-making and patient management processes if applied in clinical practice. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed model for AF risk prediction has the potential to provide valuable information in decision-making and patient management processes if applied in clinical practice. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this cautious translational potential claim with equivalent framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Astroconformer demonstrates superior performance, achieving a root-mean-square-error (RMSE) of 0.017 dex at \log g\approx3 in data-rich regimes and up to 0.1 dex in sparser areas. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Astroconformer demonstrates superior performance, achieving a root-mean-square-error (RMSE) of 0.017 dex at log g  3 in data-rich regimes and up to 0.1 dex in sparser areas. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the RMSE performance claim with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Astroconformer excels in extracting \nu_{max} with high precision, achieving less than 2% relative median absolute error for 90-day red giant light curves. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Astroconformer excels in extracting max with high precision, achieving less than 2 per cent relative median absolute error for 90-d red giant light curves. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this high-precision extraction claim with equivalent metrics and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The attention mechanisms in \textit{Astroconformer} align closely with the characteristics of stellar oscillations and granulation observed in light curves. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The attention mechanisms in Astroconformer align closely with the characteristics of stellar oscillations and granulation observed in light curves. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the interpretability conclusion about attention aligning with astrophysical signals.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Ablation studies highlight the influence of receptive field size on model effectiveness, with larger fields correlating to improved results. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Ablation studies highlight the influence of receptive field size on model effectiveness, with larger fields correlating to improved results. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.rationale: Publication language shifts to an "aims to" formulation and frames the path as mimicking traditional methods, softening the original stronger claim of offering superior runtime efficiency.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A dataset of size O˜(|S||A|ε−2p(1−γ)−(2p+2)) guarantees the supremum p-Wasserstein metric between ηˆnπ and ηπ less than ε with high probability. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same sample-complexity bound for the p-Wasserstein metric (same scaling and dependencies) using comparable notation; the result is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Under different mild assumptions, a dataset of size $\ilde O\big(\frac{|\text{S}||\text{A}|}{\text{ε}^{2}(1-\text{γ})^{4}}\big)$ suffices to ensure the Kolmogorov metric and total variation metric between $\ilde{\eta}^\pi$ and $\eta^\pi$ is below $\text{ε}$ with high probability. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same sample complexity guarantees for Kolmogorov-Smirnov and total variation metrics under similar assumptions; notation differs but the result is equivalent.

alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the work toward VAE-based federated pruning and emphasizes acceleration and performance; the explicit privacy protection emphasis from the preprint is deemphasized, so the original privacy-focused claim is revised and effectively narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method establishes a federated topic model that ensures the privacy of each node. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed federated topic model pruning based on the variational autoencoder can greatly accelerate the model training speed and inference speed while ensuring the model’s performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes speed and performance of a specific VAE-based federated pruning method and does not reiterate the strong per-node privacy guarantee worded in the preprint, so the contribution is reframed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using neural network model pruning can significantly reduce the model inference time during the inference process. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed federated topic model pruning based on the variational autoencoder can greatly accelerate the model training speed and inference speed while ensuring the model’s performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the preprint's inference-time reduction claim to stronger language (greatly accelerate) and additionally reports training-speed gains and maintained performance, thus broadening and amplifying the original result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The first pruning method ensures higher accuracy but has a limited acceleration effect on the model training process. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The first method of slow pruning throughout the entire model training process can ensure that the pruned model achieves higher accuracy, despite having a limited acceleration effect. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the characterization of the slow/throughout pruning method and its trade-off between accuracy and acceleration.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The second pruning strategy may lose more useful information but can complete model training faster. (certainty=medium, type=result)
